text:
Two Channel 9 hosts have offered two completely different experiences of Paris during their Olympic Games coverage with Karl Stefanovic's “weirdos” claim being negated by former Millionaire Hotseat presenter Eddie McGuire.

Channel 9 host Eddie McGuire has delighted in the splendours of the City of Light during his Paris Olympic Games coverage, rejecting his colleague Karl Stefanovic’s dismissive attitude having called French people “weirdos”.

The two Channel 9 hosts have offered two completely different outlooks as the Games approach the close on Sunday, with Stefanovic claiming on the Kyle and Jackie O show on Monday the French were a “weird nation, sexually”.

McGuire, on the other hand, rejected these assertions and said his experience had been “the opposite”.

“Love the vibe, people have been great, wonderful food, drivers have been hospitable,” McGuire said.

“Can’t wait to get to the end so I can have some magnificent French wine. Shops, landmarks, history, fashion (is) inspirational.

“Wonderful Rodin and Picasso exhibitions, shops and fashion totally wonderful. The games have been inspirational and aspirational. It’s everything I hoped the Paris would be. Love it, Love Paris, Viva La France.”

Stefanovic, who is know for jesting and poking fun, told the KIIS radio hosts the French had been rude and pompous, despite having an all-expenses paid luxurious trip with a front row seat of the Games.

“The French are weirdos. The French are complete weirdos. We all know that,” Stefanovic said.

“They’re kind of a weird nation, sexually.”

Stefanovic said he had “stopped trying” to learn French and came clean that he did not really “like them that much”.

“They’re so rude. Oh, they’re really up themselves... they’re pompous. They do a good burger though. I do like the burgers and stuff over here,” he said.

However, McGuire said the rule-dominated city of Melbourne needed an overhaul, and to take a leaf from the French with their ‘no rules apply’ attitude.

“When good Americans die, they go to Paris. Where do bad Americans go? They stay in America,’’ McGuire said, invoking Oscar Wilde.

“You can have all this if you want.

“I’m not a smoker but I love the French insouciance. We need to get that more into Melbourne. Too many rules. Too many people with no idea telling us how to live."

McGuire said he loved the “laissez-faire, avant garde” approach to life the French carried.

“They are French words because that is the “je ne sais quoi” of Paris,” he said.

“That’s the magic.”

title: 'Viva la France': Channel 9 host Eddie McGuire adores Paris amid Olympic trip, rejects Karl Stefanovic's 'weirdos' claim | Sky News Australia
description: Channel 9 host Eddie McGuire has delighted in the splendours of the City of Light during his Paris Olympic Games coverage, rejecting his colleague Karl Stefanovic’s dismissive attitude having called French people “weirdos”.
